title: Elution, SDS-PAGE, and RNA Purification
author: Clémentine elan-Forino
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bnthmej6

steps.title: Spin out the void volume
steps.instruction: Spin out the void volume. Close the bottom of the column with the press-on stopper and add 200 µL, Elution buffer .

steps.title: Incubate the resin On ice for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the resin On ice for 00:05:00 . Alternatively, to increase efficiency, incubation can be performed at 16 °C with shaking.

steps.title: Collect the flow-through in a 1
steps.instruction: Collect the flow-through in a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube. Reclose the bottom of the column and repeat the elution with another 200 µL, Elution buffer . Use the Geiger counter to ensure that the elution flow-through is radioactive.

steps.title: Collect the residual Elution buffer on...
steps.instruction: Collect the residual Elution buffer on the column by briefly spinning the column.

steps.title: Pool the eluates into a single microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Pool the eluates into a single microcentrifuge tube. Add 40 µg, GlycoBlue coprecipitant and 100 µL, trichloroacetic acid . Vortex and incubate On ice for 01:00:00 . Alternatively, you can elute in 2× 100 μl, add 40 μg of GlycoBlue coprecipitant and 1 ml of acetone to precipitate overnight at −20 °C (and skip step 7).

steps.title: Centrifuge at top speed for 00:30:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at top speed for 00:30:00 at 4 °C . Remove the supernatant (use the Geiger counter to ensure that the pellet has not been dislodged if the blue pellet is not visible).

steps.title: Add 800 µL, ice-cold acetone to...
steps.instruction: Add 800 µL, ice-cold acetone to the pellet and centrifuge for 00:20:00 at 4 °C . Pellets should be small and clear. If it is big and white, add additional acetone wash step and pipette up and down until the pellet has dissolved completely. Longer incubation with acetone on ice can also be considered.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and air-dry the pellet at room temperature
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and air-dry the pellet at room temperature. Pellets can be hard to resuspend; do not allow them to overdry as this can lead to loss of material during the resuspension step.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 30 µL,...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 30 µL, 1× NuPAGE LDS sample buffer (dilute 4× buffer in distilled water before use). Pipette along the wall of the tube very carefully and check with the Geiger counter to ensure that most of the material has been resuspended.

steps.title: Load the sample and SeeBlue2 ladder...
steps.instruction: Load the sample and SeeBlue2 ladder onto a NuPAGE 4–12% gradient gel. Run for 01:00:00 at maximum 150 V or until the dye reaches the bottom of the gel.

steps.title: Transfer the protein–RNA complex to nitrocellulose...
steps.instruction: Transfer the protein–RNA complex to nitrocellulose using a wet transfer western blotting system, at 100 V for 01:30:00 in NuPage transfer buffer supplemented with 10% Methanol. To avoid overheating during transfer, the tank can be placed in ice.

steps.title: Expose the membrane (wrapped in cling...
steps.instruction: Expose the membrane (wrapped in cling film or protected by a transparent plastic film) to a high-sensitivity X-ray film at -80 °C . If samples are highly radioactive, a 00:30:00 – 01:00:00 exposure time should be enough. Overnight exposure is often required for samples with weaker radioactive signal. Ensure that a chemiluminescent marker is included to realign membrane and film after developing.

steps.title: Develop the X-ray film and align...
steps.instruction: Develop the X-ray film and align it to the membrane using the chemiluminescent rulers. Cut out the smear corresponding to the size of the protein–RNA complex for all the samples. Cut at the same place in the negative control lane. Use clean scalpel for each sample. The first incision can be made in the middle of the band corresponding to the protein of interest plus the smear above to get most cross-link species. Once membrane fragments have been excised, they can be stored overnight (or longer) at -20 °C or -80 °C . An example of a radiolabeled blot for Rrp44-HTP is shown in Fig. 1b. Fig. 1 Key steps of Rrp44-HTP CRAC protocol. ( a ) Western blot to test purification of Rrp44 after lysis (Crude Lysate or CL sample) and after elution from IgG beads and TEV cleavage (TEV eluate or TE sample). Difference in size is due to cleavage of part of the tag (Protein A × 2) by TEV protease. ( b ) Autoradiogram of labeled RNAs cross-linked to HTP-Rrp44. The part of the membrane cut and subjected to proteinase K treatment is indicated in red. ( c ) cDNA library (product of PCR amplification) resolved on Metaphor agarose gel. The region of the gel that was cut for size selection and gel purification of the library is indicated in red

steps.title: To digest away proteins, incubate the...
steps.instruction: To digest away proteins, incubate the membrane slices with 400 µL, Proteinase K buffer (50 mM Tris–HCl pH 7.8, 50 mM NaCl, 0.1% NP-40, 5 mM β-mercaptoethanol, 1% SDS, 5 mM EDTA). Add 100 µg, Proteinase K and incubate at 55 °C for 02:00:00 with gentle mixing.

steps.title: Add 50 µL, 3 M sodium acetate (pH 5
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL, 3 M sodium acetate (pH 5.2) and 500 µL, phenol–chloroform–isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1) . Vortex and centrifuge for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous phase to clean...
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous phase to clean microcentrifuge tube and add 1 mL, ice cold absolute ethanol and 20 µg, GlycoBlue . Incubate at -80 °C for 00:30:00 and centrifuge at 16000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes .

steps.title: Wash the pellet with 500 µL,...
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet with 500 µL, ice cold 70% ethanol and centrifuge for 00:20:00 . Aspirate the supernatant and air dry.
